question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_CACHE_ENTITY_MAXSIZE=0 via environment variable to disable entity caching, causing every request to hit the DB and degrading performance to a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_CACHE_ENTITY_MAXSIZE to a very large integer via environment variable, causing the cache to allocate unbounded memory and triggering OOM?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_REALM or HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_SHARD to a very large int64 value via environment variable, causing integer overflow or unexpected behavior in account ID resolution?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_REALM to a negative int64 value via environment variable, causing invalid realm values to be used in account ID construction and corrupting transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_HOST to an attacker-controlled hostname via environment variable (via v.AutomaticEnv()), redirecting all DB connections to a malicious server and enabling credential harvesting?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_PORT to an unexpected uint16 value (e.g., 0 or 65535) via environment variable, causing DB connection failures and a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_NAME to an empty string or attacker-controlled value via environment variable, causing the server to connect to a wrong or nonexistent database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_USERNAME to an attacker-controlled value via environment variable, causing the server to authenticate to the DB with a different user and potentially gaining access to different DB privileges?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the v.AutomaticEnv() + v.SetEnvKeyReplacer() combination by setting environment variables with the keyDelimiter '::' pattern to inject unexpected nested config keys that viper resolves incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_FEATURE_SUBNETWORKIDENTIFIER=true via environment variable to enable the SubNetworkIdentifier feature flag, altering API behavior in ways that expose additional attack surface?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODEVERSION to an arbitrary string via environment variable, causing the server to advertise a false node version and potentially bypassing version-based validation in clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a crafted string with more than 3 colon-separated parts (e.g., '192.168.0.1:50211:0.0.3:extra') to bypass the len(parts) != 3 check and inject unexpected data into the NodeMap key construction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a string with only 2 colon-separated parts to trigger the 'Invalid node string' error path and cause LoadConfig() to return nil, resulting in a startup denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a very large comma-separated list of node entries to cause excessive memory allocation in the nodeInfoMap and trigger OOM during config loading?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a string containing only commas or whitespace to cause strings.Split() to produce empty parts, bypassing the len(parts) != 3 check and injecting empty keys into the NodeMap?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to an IPv6 address format (e.g., '::1:50211:0.0.3') to cause strings.Split(node, ':') to produce more than 3 parts, triggering an error and denying service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a value where the account ID part is '0.0.0' (zero account) to inject a zero-value hiero.AccountID into the NodeMap, causing the server to route transactions to an invalid node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a very long single node string (e.g., hostname of 1MB) to cause excessive memory allocation in fmt.Sprintf('%s:%s', parts[0], parts[1]) and trigger OOM?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user place a malicious application.yml in the current working directory (v.AddConfigPath('.')) to override config values when the server is started from an attacker-influenced directory, redirecting DB connections or disabling security features?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: '] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the silent suppression of viper.ConfigFileNotFoundError in mergeExternalConfigFile() to mask legitimate config file errors, causing the server to silently run with only default (potentially insecure) configuration?
